Low-pressure area forms over Bay of Bengal; heatwave conditions likely to Intensify in Odisha
Bhubaneswar(11/05/2026) By Ishani Chhatoraj : A low-pressure area has formed over the southwest Bay of Bengal adjoining the west-central Bay near the Sri Lanka coast, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) said on Monday.The weather system is expected to gradually intensify during the next 48 hours, though no immediate warning has been issued for Odisha so far. According to the IMD, the cyclonic circulation associated with the system currently lies over the southwest Bay of Bengal and adjoining regions. Under its influence, a low-pressure area developed over the sea region near Sri Lanka and the adjoining Tamil Nadu coast.Meteorologists stated that the system is likely to move north-westwards and may strengthen further over the coming two days. Despite the development over the Bay of Bengal, weather experts said Odisha is unlikely to witness any immediate impact in terms of heavy rainfall or storm activity.Instead, dry weather conditions are expected to continue across most parts of the state, leading to a rise in day temperatures and discomfort due to humidity. The IMD has indicated that heatwave conditions may intensify in several districts of Odisha during the next few days.Interior districts are likely to experience a sharp rise in maximum temperatures, while coastal regions may witness sultry weather conditions because of increased moisture incursion from the sea. Several places in western and interior Odisha have already been recording temperatures above normal.The mercury is expected to climb further, particularly in districts such as Sambalpur, Jharsuguda, Sundargarh, Balangir, Sonepur, and Boudh.Authorities have advised people to avoid prolonged exposure to the sun during peak afternoon hours and to remain hydrated. Weather scientists are closely monitoring the movement and intensity of the low-pressure system over the Bay of Bengal.Although Odisha remains outside the warning zone at present, officials said any significant change in the system’s trajectory or strength would be monitored carefully and updates would be issued accordingly. The formation of low-pressure systems over the Bay of Bengal during the pre-monsoon season is considered a common meteorological phenomenon. Such systems often influence moisture flow and regional weather patterns across eastern India, including Odisha.
